author: Alexander of Alexandria
book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: IV.—Epistle to Æglon, Bishop of Cynopolis, Against the Arians.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: From a letter of St. Alexander, bishop of Alexandria, to Æglon, bishop of Cynopolis, against the Arians.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Natural will is the free faculty of every intelligent nature as having nothing involuntary which is in respect of its essence.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Natural operation is the innate motion of all substance.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Natural operation is the substantial and notifying reason of every nature.

verses.book: Epistles on the Arian Heresy and the Deposition of Arius.
verses.text: Natural operation is the notifying virtue of every substance.
